authoritative_sources.title: Advertising Allowances and Other Merchandising Payments and Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC guidance implementing the Fred Meyer Guides is the authoritative U.S. public baseline for promotional allowances and merchandising support, which defines where trade-promotion operations stops and legal or finance approval begins.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: promotion funding, customer-program setup, and commercial-boundary review
used_for: evidence expectations for promotional program support

authoritative_sources.title: Guides Against Deceptive Pricing
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC deceptive-pricing guidance is the authoritative public source for promoted-price and price-comparison claim boundaries, preventing the lane from improvising legal price-claim approval.
used_for: conflict escalation when legal interpretation is required

authoritative_sources.title: FSMA Final Rule on Requirements for Additional Traceability Records for Certain Foods
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's traceability rule is the authoritative U.S. public baseline for critical tracking events and key data elements that control whether food CPG lots can be traced, released, or escalated during promotions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: lot and shipment traceability review for promoted inventory

authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 117 - Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 21 CFR Part 117 is the primary public regulatory baseline for cGMP, records, holding, distribution, and corrective-action controls that affect whether promoted food inventory can proceed safely.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: quality status, hold, release, and corrective-action review

authoritative_sources.title: Initiation of Voluntary Recalls Under 21 CFR Part 7, Subpart C
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA recall guidance is an authoritative operating reference for when promoted inventory enters recall or market-withdrawal handling and the lane must stop execution and escalate.

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 Global Traceability Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 is the recognized standards body for cross-enterprise identifiers and traceability exchanges used throughout CPG supplier, distributor, and retailer workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: item, lot, location, and event traceability handoff

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 Global Data Synchronisation Network (GDSN)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GDSN is the recognized public standard for synchronized trade-item attributes and master data used for pack, case, and item setup across CPG trading partners.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: promotion item setup and retailer master-data readiness

authoritative_sources.title: GTIN Management Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 GTIN rules are the authoritative public identifier baseline for deciding when promotional packs or materially changed trade items require distinct downstream identification.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: promotional pack setup and item identity control

workflow_stages.name: promotion program intake and readiness review
workflow_stages.description: Review deal sheets, customer requirements, event windows, item setup, accrual assumptions, and proof-of-performance obligations to determine whether the promotion is operationally ready.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: inventory, lot, and release dependency validation
workflow_stages.description: Check whether batch, lot, shipment, hold, recall, and quality records support promotion allocation and shipment without bypassing formal release authority.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: retailer claim, billback, and closeout evidence handling
workflow_stages.description: Organize proof-of-performance, item-data, shipment, and event evidence needed for retailer deductions, billbacks, and closeout while refusing unsupported legal or pricing interpretations.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: incident, recall, and supplier-quality escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate recurring defects, recall exposure, supplier substitution pressure, or unresolved nonconformance without closing the incident or approving release inside this lane.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

artifacts: promotion calendars, customer deal sheets, and event briefs
trade accrual records, billbacks, scan or deduction claims, and proof-of-performance packets
item setup records, GTIN and pack data, retailer portal forms, and synchronized product attributes
ERP orders, TPM event records, WMS allocations, and shipment confirmations
lot, batch, hold, recall, nonconformance, deviation, and corrective-action records
supplier records, substitution requests, and change-control documentation
systems_of_record: product information management or GDSN-connected item-data systems

allowed: assess whether visible promotion, inventory, lot, and proof-of-performance evidence supports a readiness summary, blocked disposition, or escalation package
organize authoritative records, missing prerequisites, and next owners for event setup, closeout, deduction support, and blocked-promotion handling
summarize traceability conflicts, hold status, and customer evidence gaps without implying final approval authority
escalate: final legal interpretation of promotional pricing, allowance, contract, or competition-law questions
final quality release, recall management, or safety disposition
supplier approval, substitution, or formal change-control authority
financial approval of promotional funding, settlements, or write-offs
refuse: override documented hold, release, recall, or traceability controls to meet a promotion date
certify legal compliance of promoted-price or promotional-allowance structures
treat incomplete lot, shipment, or proof-of-performance records as sufficient to close a retailer claim or clear product for event use

domain_failure_modes: promoted inventory is allocated from a lot with unresolved hold, recall, or deviation status
TPM, ERP, WMS, and QMS records conflict on the lot, quantity, or shipment tied to the retailer event
promotional pack or item setup changes proceed without correct GTIN or synchronized master data
retailer deduction or billback support is closed using incomplete proof-of-performance or unsupported price documentation
supplier substitutions or quality incidents are treated as commercial timing issues instead of formal quality or change-control escalations
the lane drifts into legal, finance approval, recall-close, or final release authority

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Retailer-specific deduction and promotional evidence rules
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public FTC and GS1 sources define the baseline boundaries, but each retailer's portal, scorecard, and claim-substantiation checklist must still be supplied by the tenant at runtime.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Non-food CPG variants outside FDA food-safety scope
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: The manufacturing-and-supply-chain research pack is satisfied with FDA and GS1 sources, but tenants selling non-food CPG lines must overlay product-specific safety and release rules where FDA food regulations do not govern the item.

notes: Authoritative batch, lot, inventory, and supplier records are the ERP, WMS, QMS, TPM, and retailer claim records designated by the tenant, constrained by FDA traceability and GS1 identifier standards where applicable.
The lane stops before formal quality release, supplier approval, recall disposition, legal pricing interpretation, and financial approval even when promotion timing pressure is high.
